codeReview.summary: Pass. The fix addresses the prior blocking capture-safety issues: non-stream capture now requires role == "assistant", non-empty reasoning_content, and complete tool calls before storing; streaming capture now marks SSE error objects as failed and only commits reconstructed tool calls when ID, function.name, and function.arguments fragments were observed. The OpenAI-compatible stream loop still observes before translation without mutating emitted chunks, still treats usage-only/no-choice chunks as safe, and still returns early without commit for non-SSE terminal JSON errors. The handoff and transcript evidence are meaningful: focused negative tests failed before implementation, then focused TestDeepSeek capture tests, all TestDeepSeek tests, race cache/concurrency tests, full go test ./..., git diff --check, build, clean status, and merge-base ancestry checks passed.

summary: Pass: commit 1f05bd67 is present on the active pr/codex-hang-failover branch and adequately fixes the non-stream assistant/valid-tool-call eligibility gap plus the streaming SSE-error and incomplete-tool-call cache-commit gaps while preserving normal usage-only chunks and stream output behavior.
